name: Prime-TB Cut Mother Operator
description: A mother-operator extension of Prime-TB Cut. It decomposes a theorem, law, fixed point, closed logic system, protocol, or existing conceptual operator into invariant kernels, operational atoms, runtime contracts, safety guards, explanation traces, benchmark scaffolds, and reusable operator blueprints.
core_thesis: Prime-TB Cut is not only a decomposition operator. In mother-operator mode, it functions as a machine-tool for manufacturing executable operators from verified structures.

formal_definition.mother_operator: Ω_PTB-M(X) = ManufactureOperator(Cut(X | K(X), B(X), R(X), θ_M))
formal_definition.source_object: X ∈ {theorem, law, fixed_point, closed_logic, protocol, existing_operator, framework}
formal_definition.invariant_kernel: K(X) = the preserved truth, fixed-point, conservation rule, optimality condition, or closed logical core that cannot be violated by the generated operator.
formal_definition.boundary_profile: B(X) = {precondition_boundary, state_boundary, transition_boundary, proof_boundary, failure_boundary, recomposition_boundary}
formal_definition.runtime_contract: R(X) = {inputs, outputs, activation_condition, state_variables, transition_rules, guards, traces, stopping_rules}
formal_definition.output: Ω_PTB-M(X) -> {K_X, A_X, C_X, G_X, T_X, B_X, Ω_X}
formal_definition.generated_operator: Ω_X = executable operator blueprint derived from X.

industrial_mother_machine.raw_material: verified theorem, law, fixed point, closed logic, or stable method
industrial_mother_machine.quality_control: invariant preservation, trace completeness, benchmarkability, and failure-boundary clarity

prime_tb_mother_cut.cut_site: boundary where a static truth can become a runtime behavior
prime_tb_mother_cut.post_cut_process: operator blueprint synthesis and benchmark scaffold generation

KX.name: Invariant Kernel
KX.role: The preserved core that the generated operator must never violate.

OAM.name: Operatorization Alignment Motif
OAM.role: A motif showing that a source object can be converted into runtime behavior.
OAM.rule: No invariant kernel, no operator synthesis. No safety guard, no executable cut.

OA.name: Operational Atom
OA.role: A minimal runtime unit extracted from the source object, such as a trigger, guard, action, state update, trace event, or stopping rule.

RC.name: Runtime Contract
RC.role: The contract that converts a static source into a callable operator with explicit inputs, outputs, activation conditions, guards, traces, failure modes, and stopping rules.

QC.name: Quality Control Layer
QC.role: Checks whether the generated operator is safe, interpretable, reusable, benchmarkable, and faithful to the source object.

mother_layers.name: Invariant Kernel Layer
mother_layers.description: Extracts the non-negotiable truth, fixed point, optimality preservation condition, or law-like core of the source.

mother_layers.name: Boundary Layer
mother_layers.description: Identifies where the source changes from statement to condition, condition to action, action to trace, or trace to benchmark.

mother_layers.name: Operational Atom Layer
mother_layers.description: Decomposes the source into triggers, guards, transformations, state variables, traces, and stopping rules.

mother_layers.name: Runtime Contract Layer
mother_layers.description: Builds input/output schemas, activation conditions, runtime pipeline, and recomposition rules.

mother_layers.name: Safety Guard Layer
mother_layers.description: Attaches proof obligations, invariant checks, invalid-cut conditions, and failure boundaries.

mother_layers.name: Trace and XAI Layer
mother_layers.description: Specifies what must be logged so the operator can explain why it activated, what it cut, and why the cut was safe.

mother_layers.name: Generated Operator Blueprint
mother_layers.description: Recomposes all extracted layers into an operator blueprint ready for TypeScript, JSON, benchmark, or AI runtime integration.

safety_rules.rule: If no invariant kernel is found, do not claim that a valid operator has been generated.
A cut is executable only when the safety condition is explicit or can be represented as a proof obligation.
The generated operator must preserve the truth, law, fixed point, or proven condition of the source artifact.
Every activation, cut, rejection, or state transition must emit an explanation trace.
Conceptual operatorization must be clearly separated from formally proven or empirically validated runtime behavior.

example_operatorization.before_operatorization: A principle or technique for eliminating regions that cannot contain a better global optimum.
example_operatorization.invariant_kernel: Global optimum preservation: never cut away a region that may contain the true global optimum.
example_operatorization.activation_condition: For minimization, a subregion S may be cut when lower_bound(S) >= incumbent_best_value, under a valid bounding proof.
operational_atoms: compute a valid lower bound for S
cut S only if the bound proves no improvement is possible
emit a trace explaining why the cut was safe
keep or branch S if the safety proof is insufficient

interpretation_boundaries: Prime-TB Cut Mother Operator is not a claim that every theorem automatically becomes executable software.
The mother-operator does not replace formal proof, empirical validation, or domain expertise.
Generated operators are blueprints unless implemented, tested, and benchmarked.
Conceptual runtime behavior must be labeled separately from verified runtime behavior.
Prime structure here is used as a decomposition and recomposition metaphor/framework unless numeric prime features are explicitly computed.
slogan: Prime-TB Cut cuts structures. Prime-TB Mother Cut manufactures operators.
